title: portulaca pilosa subsp. lakhonensis
intro: Portulaca pilosa subsp. lakhonensis is a subspecies of the Portulaca pilosa plant, also known as the yellow mudweed, a type of succulent that belongs to the Portulacaceae family. It has been traditionally used in Southeast Asian medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Portulaca pilosa subsp. lakhonensis include treating fever, wounds, and skin conditions, as well as having potential anti-cancer and anti-diabetic properties. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and its leaves and stems can also be consumed raw or cooked in traditional dishes.
